user_content: I'm preparing for my semester abroad at the University of California, Berkeley, and I was wondering if you could help me with some tips on what to expect during my first few weeks on campus. By the way, I submitted my application to the exchange program on February 10th and got accepted on March 20th, so I'm really excited to finally be going!

I'm heading to the US soon for a semester abroad and I need help with packing. Can you give me some general tips on what to bring and what to leave behind? By the way, I've been attending pre-departure orientation sessions at my home university every Friday since 3/27, which has been really helpful in preparing me for the journey.

I've already applied for a visa and had an interview at the US embassy on April 25th, so I'm all set with that. What kind of health insurance do I need to have, and are there any specific requirements for exchange students at UC Berkeley?
I'm glad we could work out a plan for my day. By the way, as I'm getting started, I just wanted to mention that I usually wake up at 7:30 am on weekdays, so I like to get a head start on my day.

I'll ask Allianz about the Look-Back Period and how they evaluate pre-existing conditions. Can you tell me more about the medical questionnaire and what kind of information I need to provide? Is it a detailed medical history or just a general questionnaire about my health?
I didn't think about pre-existing conditions, thanks for the reminder. For Allianz Travel Insurance, do they have a list of pre-existing conditions they don't cover or is it on a case-by-case basis?
I've heard that the Stevenage Old Town is quite charming. Have you been there and can you tell me more about what to expect?

That's really helpful, thanks! I've already booked my flight to San Francisco on April 1st, and it's scheduled to depart on August 15th. What should I do to prepare for my flight and arrival in the US?

I've already been taking an intensive English language course on weekends since March to improve my language skills, and I've seen significant progress. My language instructor, Ms. Johnson, has been very helpful and has given me valuable tips on how to navigate everyday conversations in the US. Can you tell me more about the English language support services available at UC Berkeley?

I've already got a laptop and a portable Wi-Fi hotspot, so I don't need to worry about those. What about clothing? How many outfits should I aim to pack, and are there any specific items you'd recommend for the US climate?
